title: Intelligent Computing Systems (AICS) Neural Network Lab
summary: A NumPy-only course lab sequence that builds an MNIST MLP, then extends the layer library into VGG19 image classification and neural style transfer.
instructionPreview: Implement a pure-NumPy deep learning layer library and two complete model pipelines: an MLP for MNIST classification, and a VGG19 image classifier plus neural style transfer system. No external deep learning framework (PyTorch, TensorFlow, etc.) may be used — only NumPy and SciPy.
instruction:
Implement a pure-NumPy deep learning layer library and two complete model pipelines: an MLP for MNIST classification, and a VGG19 image classifier plus neural style transfer system. No external deep learning framework (PyTorch, TensorFlow, etc.) may be used — only NumPy and SciPy.

Your working directory is `/workspace`. The project code is already present. Some directory names are in Simplified Chinese (e.g., `实验答案/实验二/` means "Lab Answers / Experiment 2"); treat them as opaque path components and use the exact paths listed below.

## Modules to Implement

The following modules need to be implemented or substantially built out:

- **MLP Layer Library** (`实验答案/实验二/exp_2_1/stu_upload/layers_1.py`): Core neural network layers for the MLP pipeline — fully connected layer with forward/backward/parameter-update, ReLU activation with forward/backward, and softmax loss with forward/backward. (~7 functions to implement)

- **MLP Training Pipeline** (`实验答案/实验二/exp_2_1/stu_upload/mnist_mlp_cpu.py`): The MNIST MLP training system — split across data loading, model construction, forward/backward orchestration, and the mini-batch training/evaluation loop. (~5 functions to implement)

- **CNN Layer Library** (`实验答案/实验三/exp_3_1/stu_upload/layers_1.py` and `layers_2.py`): Extended layer library for CNN models — same fully connected/ReLU/softmax layers plus 2D convolutional layer with padding and stride, and max pooling layer. (~9 functions to implement)

- **VGG19 Image Classifier** (`实验答案/实验三/exp_3_1/stu_upload/vgg_cpu.py`): A VGG19 deep CNN that uses the layer library to build the full 19-layer architecture, load pretrained parameters, preprocess classifier inputs, and run inference. (~4 functions to implement)

- **Style Transfer Layer Library** (`实验答案/实验三/exp_3_3/stu_upload/layers_1.py`, `layers_2.py`, `layers_3.py`): Full layer library for style transfer — basic FC/ReLU/softmax helpers, convolutional and max-pooling layers with raw and optimized paths, flatten backward, plus content/style loss layers built on Gram matrices. (~20 functions to implement)

- **Style Transfer Pipeline** (`实验答案/实验三/exp_3_3/stu_upload/exp_3_3_style_transfer.py`): End-to-end neural style transfer system — split across the style-transfer VGG builder/runtime, the Adam image optimizer, and the main optimization loop combining content and style losses. (~7 functions to implement)

## Test-Coupled Signatures

The following functions are called directly by the test suite. You must implement them with these exact signatures:

- `FullyConnectedLayer.forward(self, input)` — compute linear transformation, store `self.input` and `self.output`, return output
- `FullyConnectedLayer.backward(self, top_diff)` — compute `self.d_weight` and `self.d_bias` gradients, return bottom_diff
- `FullyConnectedLayer.update_param(self, lr)` — apply SGD update to weight and bias
- `FullyConnectedLayer.get_gradient(self)` — return (self.d_weight, self.d_bias)
- `FullyConnectedLayer.save_param(self)` — return (self.weight, self.bias)
- `ReLULayer.forward(self, input)` — apply activation, store `self.input`, return result
- `ReLULayer.backward(self, top_diff)` — compute element-wise gradient, return bottom_diff
- `SoftmaxLossLayer.forward(self, input)` — compute numerically stable softmax, store `self.prob`, return probabilities
- `SoftmaxLossLayer.get_loss(self, label)` — compute cross-entropy loss using `self.prob` and label
- `SoftmaxLossLayer.backward(self)` — compute loss gradient, return bottom_diff
- `ConvolutionalLayer.forward(self, input)` — compute 2D convolution, store `self.input_pad` and `self.output`, return output
- `MaxPoolingLayer.forward(self, input)` — compute max pooling, store `self.output`, return output
- `FlattenLayer.forward(self, input)` — reshape feature map from [N,C,H,W] to flattened output

moduleDag.project: Intelligent Computing Systems (AICS) Neural Network Lab
moduleDag.description: A NumPy-only course lab sequence that builds an MNIST MLP, then extends the layer library into VGG19 image classification and neural style transfer.

nodes.label: MLP Layer Library
nodes.description: Pure-NumPy fully connected, ReLU, and softmax-loss layers for the MNIST MLP lab.

nodes.label: MLP Training Data Loading
nodes.description: Loads MNIST image and label files and assembles the train/test arrays used by the MLP pipeline.

nodes.label: MLP Model Graph
nodes.description: Constructs the three-layer MLP and wires the forward/backward pass over the layer objects.

nodes.label: MLP Training / Evaluation Loop
nodes.description: Runs mini-batch training, checkpointing, and test-set evaluation for the MNIST MLP.

nodes.label: CNN Layer Library
nodes.description: Extends the layer library with convolution, pooling, and flatten support for the Experiment 3 CNN lab.

nodes.label: VGG19 Architecture Builder
nodes.description: Constructs the full VGG19 classification architecture from the Experiment 3 CNN layers.

nodes.label: VGG19 Pretrained Weight Loader
nodes.description: Loads the MatConvNet VGG19 checkpoint, including image mean extraction and weight reshaping.

nodes.label: VGG19 Image Preprocessing
nodes.description: Loads and preprocesses classification images into the classifier’s NumPy tensor layout.

nodes.label: VGG19 Inference Path
nodes.description: Runs full VGG19 inference and reports the final top-1 classification result.

nodes.label: Style Transfer Basic Layers
nodes.description: Reimplements the core fully connected, ReLU, and softmax-loss layers for the style-transfer lab.

nodes.label: Style Transfer Convolution (Raw)
nodes.description: Adds the naive forward and backward convolution implementations used as the baseline style-transfer kernels.

nodes.label: Style Transfer Convolution (Speedup)
nodes.description: Adds the accelerated convolution forward/backward path built around columnized tensors.

nodes.label: Style Transfer Pooling Layers
nodes.description: Implements raw and accelerated max-pooling behavior, including backward routing through the max locations.

nodes.label: Style Transfer Flatten Backward
nodes.description: Reshapes flattened gradients back into the feature-map layout used by the style-transfer network.

nodes.label: Style Transfer Content Loss
nodes.description: Computes the content loss and its gradient between the transfer features and the target content features.

nodes.label: Style Transfer Style Loss
nodes.description: Builds Gram matrices for style features and computes the corresponding style-loss gradient.

nodes.label: Style Transfer VGG Builder
nodes.description: Builds the style-transfer VGG19 feature extractor on top of the accelerated layer variants.

nodes.label: Style Transfer VGG Runtime
nodes.description: Loads pretrained VGG weights, handles style-transfer image I/O, and runs the forward/backward feature extraction paths.

nodes.label: Style Transfer Adam Optimizer
nodes.description: Applies Adam updates to the transfer image using the accumulated first and second moments.

nodes.label: Style Transfer Main Loop
nodes.description: Runs the top-level neural style-transfer optimization loop over content/style losses and Adam updates.

edges.label: The MLP graph imports the fully connected, ReLU, and softmax-loss layers from `layers_1.py`.
The training loop consumes the prepared train/test arrays assembled during data loading.
The training loop depends on the layer graph plus the forward/backward traversal.
Experiment 3 reuses the same FC/ReLU/Softmax patterns introduced in the MLP layer lab.
The VGG19 classifier is assembled entirely from the Experiment 3 CNN layer library.
Weights can only be loaded after the VGG19 layer objects exist.
Image preprocessing uses the dataset mean extracted from the VGG19 checkpoint.
Inference consumes the preprocessed classifier input tensor.
The style-transfer experiment reimplements the same basic FC/ReLU/Softmax layer contracts used in earlier labs.
The style-transfer convolution library extends the Experiment 3 CNN layer semantics with backward passes.
The accelerated convolution path builds on the raw convolution semantics and tensor layout.
The pooling layers are part of the same accelerated style-transfer feature extractor stack.
Flatten backward depends on the style-transfer feature-map layout established by the pooling stack.
The style-loss layer follows the content-loss layer in the course progression for Experiment 3.3.
The style-transfer VGG uses the same ReLU layer implementation from the basic style-transfer layer library.
The style-transfer VGG builder instantiates the accelerated pooling layer implementation.
The style-transfer VGG feature extractor is an extension of the inference-only VGG19 classifier.
Runtime helpers load parameters and run forward/backward passes over the constructed style-transfer VGG.
The optimizer updates the transfer image after the VGG runtime produces gradients.
The main loop depends on the VGG runtime for feature extraction, image I/O, and backpropagation.
The main loop computes content losses through the dedicated content-loss layer.
The main loop computes style losses through the Gram-matrix style-loss layer.
The main loop updates the transfer image through the Adam optimizer each step.
